sources: Manufacturing Dive: data center cycle commentary

estimate_artifact_check.explanation: The bundle's eps_ntm=9.42 and the 32-analyst consensus eps_ntm_consensus=8.95 differ by ~5.3%. The pe_ntm=34.27 in the bundle is computed from the yfinance eps_ntm (9.42), not from street consensus. At $322.86 / $8.95 = 36.1x. Daedalus should use the 36.1x figure as the consensus-derived NTM multiple. The yfinance eps_ntm=9.42 may reflect a forward year blend or non-GAAP adjustment; it cannot be reconciled without the underlying yfinance period definition. Both fields are reported; the 32-analyst consensus figure (8.95) is the market-standard reference.
estimate_artifact_check.fcf_artifact_note: FY2025 FCF = $2.60B (14.7% FCF margin). Consensus FCF/share for CY2026 is approximately $6.92 (third-party sources). Management FCF/share framework implies >$8/share if CY2026 demand trends continue. At $322.86 price, 2026 consensus FCF yield = 2.1% ($6.92/$322.86). The FCF compression in FY2023-FY2025 is entirely capex-driven (LFAB/SM1 build). FCF should normalize sharply as 2026 capex guidance of $2-3B represents a roughly 40% step-down from FY2023 peak.

consensus_estimates.note_on_target_vs_price: Consensus mean target $294.41 is 8.8% BELOW current price $322.86. Consensus median $300 is 7.1% below. The stock has run significantly through the consensus target range since the Q1-2026 earnings beat. The most recent raises (Citi $345, BofA $370, Seaport $400) sit above price; the older, unrevised targets drag the consensus mean below. This is a lagging-consensus situation.

fy2026.note: Revenue estimate ~$21B implies ~19% growth from FY2025 $17.68B. EPS $8.95 = 32-analyst consensus. FCF/share $6.92 from third-party consensus vs management framework of >$8.

fy2027.note: Revenue estimate ~$23B (+10% vs FY2026). EPS ~$9 at current consensus. Further FCF per share expansion expected as capex normalizes to $1.5-2B range.

forward_estimates.source_caveat: FY2026/FY2027 estimates from web search aggregation (multiple sources). Not in Hermes bundle. Flag for Daedalus: treat as approximate range, not point estimates.

earnings_reaction.result: BEAT on both revenue and EPS
earnings_reaction.stock_reaction: Stock rose ~7% on the day (April 23, 2026) on the beat-and-raise; described by multiple sources as 'best-ever Q1' by management
earnings_reaction.source: Quiver Quantitative; Investing.com earnings call transcript summary; WebSearch aggregation

rating_and_pt_changes.note: Named TXN top semiconductor pick; cited product price increases and TI's expanding data center power market share
Said multi-year content gains underappreciated; industrial and auto 'have finally turned the corner'; cited 'transformative' AI data center growth
Highest PT on the Street as of freeze date. Bullish on cycle recovery and manufacturing moat
33% PT increase immediately post-Q1; reflects earnings recovery and cycle inflection
Maintained cautious stance despite raising PT; Equal Weight signals valuation concern even with cycle recovery
Part of broad post-Q1 PT lift wave
Part of broad post-Q1 PT lift wave

rating_and_pt_changes.firm: Broad wave (Goldman Sachs, TD Cowen, Benchmark, KeyBanc, Rosenblatt, Jefferies, Truist, Stifel, BofA, Barclays, Mizuho, Aletheia)
rating_and_pt_changes.rating_change: BofA, Barclays, Mizuho, Aletheia moved to more positive ratings
rating_and_pt_changes.note: A wall of raises post-Q1. Multiple firms, simultaneous direction. This is a hard market fact: the post-print analyst tape was overwhelmingly bullish.
rating_and_pt_changes.source: Yahoo Finance investment story article; WebSearch aggregation

post_earnings_analyst_actions.tape_summary: At least 15+ firms raised price targets within days of the Q1 2026 print on April 22. At least 4 firms upgraded ratings. The range of new targets spans $250 (Cantor, bear end) to $400 (Seaport, bull end). The mean consensus target at $294 lags the price because older unrevised estimates anchor the average below recent raises. The direction of the tape is unambiguously bullish: no downgrades or material target cuts in the April-June period.

street_thesis: TXN is the structural beneficiary of the analog/embedded inventory correction ending, with three reinforcing drivers: (1) industrial demand recovering from a severe de-stocking trough with Q1-2026 industrial revenue up 30%+ YoY; (2) a new and fast-growing data center power and connectivity revenue stream, up ~90% YoY in Q1, that was not in prior models; (3) a manufacturing model built around 300mm internal fabs (LFAB, SM1, SM2) that will structurally lower costs and lift gross margins as utilization scales, with capex normalizing from $5B peak to $2-3B in 2026. FCF per share is expected to re-rate sharply as capex rolls off. The Silicon Labs acquisition ($7.5B, all-cash, expected H1-2027 close) strengthens embedded processing and wireless connectivity.

capex_guidance.quote: CapEx for 2026 is targeted at $2 billion to $3 billion, with a growing focus on internalizing back-end assembly and test given identified capacity bottlenecks in the market
capex_guidance.note: This is TXN's own capex, not customer capex. It represents a ~40-50% step-down from FY2023 peak capex of $5.07B. The key demand-curve datapoints for Theia are hyperscaler and industrial customer capex, which are not available in the Hermes bundle.

capex_guidance_note_for_theia: The Hermes bundle contains no customer capex data (SEC fetch failed). Data center revenue grew ~90% YoY in Q1-2026, but the absolute data center revenue figure is not segmented in TXN's public filings — it is disclosed as an end-market comment, not a formal segment. Hyperscaler capex levels relevant to TXN's data center power business are not in the bundle. Theia will need to source these independently.

sentiment.institutional_ownership_note: 92.6% institutional is typical for large-cap quality semis. No major institutional buying/selling data in bundle. Short interest of ~2% is low — does not suggest a significant short overhang.

decomposition.interpretation: At 36x NTM EPS, the market is pricing TXN well above its through-cycle mid-cycle P/E (analog semis typically trade 20-25x mid-cycle). The premium has two components: (1) cyclical recovery — EPS is still below peak ($9.41 in FY2022), so the market is looking through a trough multiple; (2) structural re-rate — data center demand and the 300mm manufacturing model imply a structurally higher FCF margin at cycle normalization, justifying a higher steady-state multiple.

gordon_growth_implied.verdict: A 36x P/E or ~47x CY2026 FCF at r=10% implies approximately 7.9% long-run FCF growth. This is aggressive for a cyclical analog semi. The implied terminal g is only sustainable if (a) the data center power segment continues to scale for many years and (b) capex normalization permanently widens FCF margins vs history.

decomposition.what_is_priced_in: The market prices a structural inflection, not just a cyclical recovery. Specifically: continued data center revenue growth, capex normalization driving FCF per share well above $8 by 2027-2028, industrial and auto end-markets holding at or above Q1-2026 run rates, and the 300mm manufacturing moat translating into durable gross margin expansion to 59-62%. A reversion to pre-data-center demand or a capex restart would break the thesis.

market_view.whats_priced_in: The current price of $322.86 (36x NTM EPS of $8.95) prices a structural earnings inflection, not merely cyclical recovery. The market expects: (1) FCF per share to recover well past the $5.74 FY2022 peak as capex normalizes; (2) gross margins to expand to the high-50s to low-60% range on 300mm utilization scale; (3) data center power becoming a durable new revenue stream. A mean-reversion of demand or a capex restart would require meaningful multiple de-rating.
market_view.street_view: Bulls argue TXN is in the early innings of a multi-year FCF per share expansion cycle, driven by three independent drivers (industrial recovery, data center build-out, and capex normalization). Bears argue the stock has already priced the entire recovery at 36x NTM earnings, and that the Silicon Labs deal ($7.5B all-cash) adds integration risk and leverage to an already-stressed balance sheet. The tape since Q1 earnings is overwhelmingly bullish — over 15 target raises, at least 4 upgrades, zero downgrades.
market_view.post_earnings_analyst_actions_summary: Q1-2026 (April 22, 2026): revenue +6.9% vs consensus, EPS +22.6% vs consensus. At least 15+ analyst firms raised price targets in the weeks following the print. Seaport upgraded to Buy at $400. BofA raised to $370. Citi raised to $345 (June 15, after product price increase news). No downgrades in the April-June period. The consensus mean target of $294 lags the price because pre-earnings targets have not all been refreshed.
market_view.news_narrative: Q1-2026 was described as the company's best-ever Q1 by management, driven by industrial up ~30% and data center up ~90% year-over-year. The stock rose ~7% on April 23. The beat was genuine (not a guide-down-then-beat): Q2 guidance of $5.0-5.4B exceeded prior consensus of ~$4.8B. The narrative is a cyclical-recovery-plus-structural-growth story, not a beat-but-flat-guidance situation. The Silicon Labs acquisition announcement (February 4, 2026) adds scale to embedded processing and wireless connectivity; synergies of ~$450M expected within three years post-close (expected H1 2027).
market_view.sentiment_narrative: Social sentiment data unavailable (StockTwits HTTPError in bundle). Short interest ~2% — not a contrarian signal. Institutional ownership 92.6%. The stock is up ~84% YTD and ~67% over one year, trading near its 52-week high of $331.51 — momentum is strong. No insider selling data in bundle.
market_view.runway: The stock is trading within 3% of its 52-week high at $322.86 vs $331.51 high. Consensus mean target of $294 is below price, but the most-recently-revised targets (Seaport $400, BofA $370, Citi $345) imply 7-24% upside from current levels. The next catalyst is Q2-2026 earnings (July 22, 2026). Q2 guidance implies $5.0-5.4B revenue vs Q1's $4.83B — sequential growth would further validate the recovery thesis.
market_view.runway_verdict: VALUATION RISK IS REAL AND DISTINCT FROM THE RUNWAY. The fundamental demand runway (industrial recovery + data center growth) appears intact based on Q1 evidence. However, the multiple at 36x NTM P/E is pricing significant further FCF growth beyond 2026. The separation is: the business is fundamentally in recovery (positive runway), but the valuation already reflects optimistic assumptions (valuation risk is HIGH). A priced-for-perfection dynamic exists at the current price level.

market_lean.basis: Post-Q1 analyst tape is overwhelmingly bullish (15+ raises, 4 upgrades, 0 downgrades). Fundamental recovery is data-confirmed (Q1 revenue +19% YoY, industrial +30%, data center +90%). Stock momentum is strong (up 84% YTD). Short interest is low. However, the 36x NTM multiple and stock trading near 52-week highs mean the bull case is substantially priced. The residual uncertainty is whether data center demand is durable (not a temporary hyperscaler cycle) and whether the Silicon Labs deal integrates smoothly.

end_market_commentary.automotive_growth_seq: approximately flat (China auto down, rest-of-world up)
end_market_commentary.personal_electronics: not specifically called out in available sources

q1_2026_operational_facts.inventory_trend: Down from 222 days previous quarter; management targets low end of 150-250 day range during upturn

q2_2026_guidance.implied_gross_margin_commentary: management guided low-to-mid 59% gross margin for Q2 vs 58% in Q1

silicon_labs_acquisition.rationale: Adds embedded wireless connectivity (IoT) portfolio to TXN's analog and embedded processing; combined entity to be global leader in embedded wireless connectivity
silicon_labs_acquisition.eps_accretion: accretive to EPS in first full year post-close (excluding transaction costs)
silicon_labs_acquisition.note: This is TXN's largest acquisition since National Semiconductor ($6.5B, 2011). All-cash structure will increase net debt by ~$7.5B. Net debt post-close would reach approximately $18B vs $10.8B at freeze date. This is a material balance sheet change Daedalus must factor into the valuation.
